response.justification: Candidate has strong general software engineering breadth (web, backend, some embedded-adjacent C/C++ work at National Instruments and a C++ bachelor thesis), but the role demands native-level Japanese, deep embedded Linux/kernel modification experience, and specialized networking-security knowledge (MACsec, 802.1X, L2/L3 switching, NETCONF/SNMP). None of these are evidenced in the resume — Japanese is not listed, recent experience is web/SaaS/AI tooling in TypeScript, and C is not a primary current skill. The hard requirement on Japanese alone is disqualifying.
strengths: Long, diverse engineering career with TDD, CI/CD, and craftsmanship focus
Some C/C++ background at National Instruments and a Linux/C++ bachelor thesis
Strong open-source and community contribution track record
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency listed (hard requirement is native-level)
No embedded firmware, LAN-switch, MACsec, 802.1X, NETCONF/SNMP, or Linux kernel modification experience
Recent work is web/TypeScript/AI tooling — far from embedded C and switch security

key_factors.factor: General SWE breadth and some C/Linux background
